rationale: A precision strike will exploit enemy overextension and collapsing morale, delivering a decisive blow before diplomacy gains momentum.
Forward-deploying naval assets in the disputed strait reinforces our dominance and threatens key adversary supply lines.
A rapid, shallow incursion will seize tactically vital ground and demonstrate our resolve to reclaim historically rightful territory.
Disrupting Tethys’ critical infrastructure will exploit their weakening military cohesion and amplify economic strain without triggering open escalation.
Covertly sabotaging key supply depots will complement our military’s limited strike by degrading enemy logistics at a crucial moment.
Fabricating evidence of Tethys preparing WMD use will undermine their international support and justify our actions to neutral powers.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to offset sanction-driven deficits and fund critical military-industrial expansion.
Engaging neutral states in trade talks will diversify supply routes and weaken the cohesion of the sanctioning coalition.
Devaluing our currency relative to Tethys undermines their export competitiveness and amplifies their economic instability.
We must harden our frontlines in the western corridor to blunt Novaris’s anticipated offensive and deny them quick gains.
Increasing vigilance along contested sectors will disrupt enemy reconnaissance and deter surprise maneuvers.
Proactive intelligence gathering is essential to anticipate enemy movements and counter their buildup before it escalates.
We must urgently neutralize Novaris’s infiltration networks to protect our military deployments and prevent sabotage ahead of their western offensive.
Strengthening our critical systems against Novaris’s cyber intrusions is essential to maintain command integrity during the coming escalation.
Deepening our human and signals intelligence on enemy troop movements will provide early warning and targeting opportunities for defensive operations.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to sustain defense spending and stabilize public confidence amid economic collapse.
Negotiating emergency trade corridors with neutral states can bypass sanctions and secure critical imports without provoking further escalation.
Strategically devaluing our currency will boost export competitiveness and generate foreign exchange reserves to fund essential wartime imports.
With 75% international support, we must institutionalize our position by convening a formal multilateral forum led by neutral states to legitimize peace efforts and isolate Novaris diplomatically.
Strengthening our diplomatic alignment with Tethys ensures unified messaging and maximizes pressure on Novaris within international institutions.
Deploying observers to conflict zones reinforces transparency, deters further aggression, and bolsters our credibility amid escalating military and humanitarian risks.
